search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae abnormal liver tests guideline UAE
site:bsg.org.uk abnormal liver blood tests previous results clinical context history referral
candidate_sources_rejected: abnormal enzyme magnitude used to infer disease severity
NAFLD or hepatitis guidance used to assign the cause
automatic liver screen, ultrasound, medicine change, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The exact BSG guidance states that magnitude alone does not determine clinical significance.
Cause-specific guidance requires a confirmed context.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children with abnormal liver blood tests as covered by the BSG guideline; pregnancy, acute liver failure, known chronic liver disease, toxic exposure, and unstable illness need tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact BSG section requires review of previous results, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact clinical-history section supports clinician-entered jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, medicine, travel, exposure, and alcohol context plus assessed examination and concern.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact standard-panel section supports recording reviewed bilirubin and liver blood-test components without interpreting a patient result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of bilirubin and standard liver blood-test components; it does not provide patient-specific interpretation or diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact response pathway supports clinician-entered investigation, referral, safety-net, and follow-up status without generating an action or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.

unresolved_source_gaps: Specific analyte, value, trend, synthetic function, pregnancy, acute illness, cause, imaging, serology, medicine, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
